title: Automated-writer isolation & non-destructive closeout — changeset-scoped integration, PR-flow adoption (lane/* transport), observe-only main
summary: Prep research for decision #1985 (RESOLVED 2026-06-30: Rung 1 ratified as a forced invariant; Rung 2 DIRECTION ratified — adopt PR-flow / multi-session; Rung 3 folded into the rollout mechanism, prepared as #1996). Extends the resolved #1933 clone model; we:reports/2026-06-30-automated-writer-isolation.md. A real incident (batch-2026-06-29e): a /workflow closeout ran in the SHARED primary checkout, diffed the DIRTY working tree to infer 'its own residue', mis-read a concurrent /prepare session's in-progress #1983 files as residue, and git-checkout/rm'd them (no loss only by luck) — we:backlog/1985-…:17-35, we:.claude/agent-memory/closeout-never-infers-ownership-from-dirty-tree.md. IN-REPO GROUNDING: #1933 clone model is built+proven (lanes = clones pushing lane/* refs, central integrator merges to main one-at-a-time with full gate, rebase-retry-never-force; proven WE-only batch-2026-06-29b 5/6, 0 stranded — but lane refs can fail-to-push and fall back to serial replay, parallel-workflow-blocked-by-git-guard.md:53-57). The #1153 branch guard denies git switch/worktree-add/checkout -b in the shared checkout (forced clones over worktrees); push to main now allowed, broad-stage git add -A still denied. Rule 105: claim ignores git state (ownership = status:active, never the tree). #083 ratified the repo coordinates via item-level status claims + worktrees/clones, routing AROUND file locks. Rung-3 blocker: the vite proxy is a hard-coded allowlist (#210) and .fui-card lands cross-origin only where the FUI registration ESM loads (#1895, blockedBy #1982), so per-lane visual rendering needs WE+FUI origins up and wired per lane ('the constellation, not one --port'). The PR-flow rollout mechanism is now prepared as #1996 (the per-lane visual harness = its Gate 5). PRIOR ART (SE practice): git worktrees share the object store/refs (only HEAD/index per-worktree) and refuse to check out a ref twice — a full clone avoids both (F1/F2, git-scm.com); worktrees give CODE isolation not RUNTIME isolation, shared object store/DB can still corrupt (F3/F7, blog-level) — why rung 3 needs full clones+ports not just a branch; trunk-based dev = short-lived single-owner branches converging to main via a review+CI gate (F4/F5, trunkbaseddevelopment.com, atlassian.com) — the faithful map for lane/* as proto-PR units; 2024-26 AI-agent norm gives each agent its own working dir+index (F6, blog-level); GitHub branch protection / GitLab 'Allowed to push: No one' ARE the institutional observe-only main (F8-F10, docs.github.com, docs.gitlab.com); Terraform state is 'a snapshot after the last apply, NOT a live representation' and plan→apply enacts exactly the recorded diff idempotently (F11/F12, developer.hashicorp.com) — the canonical 'act on your recorded changeset, never read ambient live state as truth', exactly rung 1. SYNTHESIS (3 archetypes; RESOLVED 2026-06-30): Rung 1 (non-destructive changeset-scoped closeout) = FORCED INVARIANT — the dirty-tree-ownership branch is broken (destroyed real work), Terraform plan→apply is the industry-standard shape, RATIFIED; Rung 2 (operating model for the constellation's writers) = LIVE OPERATING-MODEL FORK, DIRECTION RATIFIED — adopt PR-flow / multi-session (clone → push → gate → review → merge through the remote), NOT 'settled by #1933' (the incident alone doesn't justify it — Rung 1 removes the harm — so it stands on the multi-session operating-model goal, which deliberately supersedes #1933's solo-dev scope); lane/* push is the general primitive (reliable via #1995); the rollout MECHANISM (clone scope, per-lane dev-server ports, landing gate, branch protection) is prepared as #1996; Rung 3 (observe-only main for visual work via per-lane multi-origin dev server + headless Playwright) = FOLDS INTO Rung 2's mechanism (#1996 Gate 5) — observe-only main is the end state of PR-flow; carried forward as a VALIDATION-GATE verdict NOT-YET, concrete un-gate trigger = a lane boots WE+FUI cross-origin and headless Playwright reproduces the #1895 transparent-.fui-card regression AND its fix from the CLI (the #210/#1895 cross-origin FUI wiring is the real gating cost), NOT a #1620 soft-park.
